examples.title: Basic Pipe Example
examples.description: Pipeline to find processes related to 'bash'
examples.code: ps aux | grep bash | grep -v grep
examples.explanation: ps aux lists all processes, first grep filters for 'bash', second grep -v removes the grep process itself from output

examples.title: Word Frequency Counter
examples.description: Count word occurrences in a file using pipeline
examples.code: cat document.txt | tr -cs 'a-zA-Z' '\n' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20
examples.explanation: tr converts to words (one per line), sort groups duplicates, uniq -c counts, final sort reverses numeric order, head shows top 20

examples.title: Email Extraction
examples.description: Extract email addresses from a file using grep
examples.explanation: -E enables extended regex, -o extracts only matching portions, sort -u shows unique sorted results

examples.title: Log Error Analysis
examples.description: Analyze log file for errors and warnings with context
examples.explanation: Shows line numbers (-n), 2 lines before (-B2) and after (-A2) each match, piped to less for navigation

examples.title: Case-Insensitive Search
examples.description: Search for pattern case-insensitively in all files recursively
examples.explanation: -r recursive, -i case-insensitive, --include='*.txt' only .txt files, 2>/dev/null suppresses permission errors
